name: Senda del Oso, from Villanueva to Proaza
name_original: Senda del Oso, de Villanueva a Proaza
region_intro: Hiking around Santo Adriano offers diverse landscapes, characterized by the Trubia Valley, surrounding mountains like Grandamiana, and significant river ecosystems. The region features dramatic limestone gorges, such as the Desfiladero de Las Xanas, and lush forests of oak and chestnut trees. These physical features provide varied terrain for outdoor activities, from gentle riverside paths to more challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Santo Adriano The most popular hiking route is Senda del Oso, from Villanueva to Proaza, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route follows an old mining railway line and passes by a bear enclosure. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Xanas – Pedroveya loop from Villanueva, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail navigates the spectacular Las Xanas Gorge, featuring narrow paths and rock-carved tunnels. Local hikers also love the Route of the Xanas – Pedroveya loop from Peñerúes, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail leading through the dramatic Las Xanas Gorge, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Santo Adriano is defined by river valleys, limestone gorges, and forested hills, with options ranging from easy, family-friendly paths to more difficult ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 3 easy, 7 moderate, and 4 difficult routes. The routes in Santo Adriano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore Santo Adriano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Santo Adriano?
A: Santo Adriano offers a diverse network of 14 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 3 easy, 7 moderate, and 4 difficult options.

Q: What makes hiking in Santo Adriano unique?
A: Hiking in Santo Adriano is unique due to its varied landscapes, including the Trubia Valley, impressive peaks like Grandamiana, and significant river ecosystems. The region is part of the 'Valleys of the Bear' and features dramatic limestone gorges, such as the Desfiladero de Las Xanas, alongside lush forests of oak and chestnut trees. You can experience everything from gentle riverside paths to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Santo Adriano?
A: The trails in Santo Adriano are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural beauty, the well-maintained paths, and the unique attractions like the bear enclosure on the Senda del Oso and the dramatic scenery of the Las Xanas Gorge.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or a relaxed walk?
A: Yes, Santo Adriano offers 3 easy hiking routes. A great option for a relaxed walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048395550/senda-del-oso-from-villanueva-to-proaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso, from Villanueva to Proaza&lt;/a&gt;. This path is built on an old mining railway line, making it relatively flat and accessible, perfect for a leisurely stroll.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Santo Adriano?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Santo Adriano features 4 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de los Pícaros – Peñas Juntas Gorge loop from Proaza&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 20 kilometers and includes significant elevation changes, offering a more demanding experience.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Santo Adriano suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails in Santo Adriano are family-friendly. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048395550/senda-del-oso-from-villanueva-to-proaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt; is particularly well-suited for families, being mostly flat and offering attractions like the bear enclosure. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20497358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Xanas&lt;/a&gt; is also popular, though some narrow sections with drops require extra caution with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the trails in Santo Adriano?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in Santo Adriano, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048395550/senda-del-oso-from-villanueva-to-proaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt;. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers, and be mindful of any specific local regulations or protected areas where dogs might be restricted.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Santo Adriano's trails offer numerous natural attractions. Along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048395550/senda-del-oso-from-villanueva-to-proaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6736914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cercado Osero (bear enclosure)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2949141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Peñas Juntas&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20497358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Xanas&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/297709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Las Xanas&lt;/a&gt;, known for its narrow paths and rock-carved tunnels. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5774152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guanga Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Peñona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Santo Adriano?
A: Yes, Santo Adriano offers several circular hiking routes. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20497358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Xanas – Pedroveya loop from Villanueva&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31710006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Xanas – Pedroveya loop from Peñerúes&lt;/a&gt;, both providing a complete scenic experience through the gorge.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Santo Adriano?
A: For popular routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048395550/senda-del-oso-from-villanueva-to-proaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20497358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Xanas&lt;/a&gt;, there are designated recreational areas that offer parking. The recreational areas of Tuñón and Buyera are convenient starting points for the Senda del Oso, while El Molín de Las Xanas near Villanueva provides parking for the Las Xanas Route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santo Adriano?
A: The best time to hike in Santo Adriano is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Spring brings lush greenery and blooming flora, while autumn offers spectacular fall colors. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the shaded gorges, but it can be warmer and more crowded. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for colder temperatures and potential wet conditions.

Q: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the trails?
A: Yes, the recreational areas near popular trailheads often have amenities. For instance, the recreational areas of Tuñón and Buyera, common starting points for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048395550/senda-del-oso-from-villanueva-to-proaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, provide picnic areas. The village of Villanueva, near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20497358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Xanas&lt;/a&gt;, also offers local establishments where you can find refreshments and meals.
tips: Nice views from the lookout above.

It was installed in the sixties, designed and carried out by the Asturian artist Joaquín Vaquero Palacios. On the side façade, Vaquero Turios, Joaquín's son, was in charge of creating a mural with geometric motifs.

Its strategic location made it a key point in the communication between Asturias and the Meseta in the Middle Ages. Proaza preserves abundant and striking vestiges from that period, such as the medieval Tower of Proaza. Proaza is a very popular place with hikers and cyclists who follow the Senda del Oso, one of the most outstanding green and ecological routes in the area.

An old fortification dating from the fifteenth century located in the town of Campo de Caso. It is located on a hill overlooking the village and offers panoramic views of the surroundings. The tower was built in medieval times as part of a defensive system to protect the area from invasions by Moors and other dangers. The tower has been restored in recent years and is in very good condition.
